mappings.reason: Performing traffic filtering between network segments directly implements the policy enforcement point mechanism required by NO-01 to control network traffic flow.
Implementing and managing a firewall on servers establishes a policy enforcement point for server traffic, satisfying a subset of NO-01's network-wide enforcement requirement.
Implementing and managing a host-based firewall on end-user devices establishes a policy enforcement point for endpoint traffic, satisfying a subset of NO-01's network-wide enforcement requirement.
Using DNS filtering services enforces policy on DNS traffic through a specific enforcement point, covering a protocol-specific subset of NO-01's network traffic requirement.
Enforcing network-based URL filters establishes a policy enforcement point for web traffic, covering a protocol-specific subset of NO-01's network traffic requirement.
Requiring remote devices to utilize a VPN establishes a policy enforcement point for remote network access traffic, satisfying a subset of NO-01's untrusted network requirement.
Deploying a Network Intrusion Prevention Solution (NIPS) enforces traffic blocking based on threats, functioning as a policy enforcement point subset of NO-01's rule-based enforcement.
Deploying port-level access control (e.g., 802.1X) enforces network admission policy at the connection point, satisfying a subset of NO-01's policy enforcement objective.
Performing application layer filtering establishes a policy enforcement point for application traffic, covering a layer-specific subset of NO-01's network traffic requirement.
